ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-24-2025 Continued To Oct-28-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-24-2025 Continued To Jul-29-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Without Prejudice (Paga)
Off Calendar Apr-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Zoe Yuzna, Esq. 323-435-9165 Colby Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Nicole Clowdsley, Esq. 415-352-6450 Freeman Mathis & Gary LLP for Defendant.

Argued. The Court announces it received a late notice to contest. Plaintiff's counsel Ms. Yuzna seeks clarification from the Court. Court and counsel discuss PAGA requirements. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ADEENA ELLISON's Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Without Prejudice.

Adeena Ellison's unopposed Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Without Prejudice (PAGA) is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Plaintiff fails to identify a statutory or common law basis for her motion. Plaintiff must identify the legal basis for her request, the applicable legal basis for adjudication and an analysis thereon. If the motion is made under Code of Civil Procedure section 581(b)(1), the court will not authorize dismissal absent more information, in light of the fact that dismissal under this statute generally does not require a court order. If the motion is Labor Code section 2699(s), the court needs much more information and analysis. "The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part." (Lab. Code, section 2699(s)(2).) Here, Plaintiff, who accepted the role of public prosecutor for purposes of the representative claim(s), must (at the very least) submit the settlement agreement for the individual claim(s) and explain how dismissal of the representative claims is in the public interest.

Section 2699(s)(2) establishes another precondition to judicial approval of a PAGA settlement: "The proposed settlement shall be submitted to the agency at the same time that it is submitted to the court." This request for dismissal of the representative claim(s) is based on settlement of Plaintiff's individual claim. Plaintiff here has not demonstrated that she submitted her papers to the LWDA. It is unlikely the court will approve dismissal of the representative claims on these grounds on a subsequent motion absent notice, and proof thereof, to the LWDA. Notice should be provided consistent with Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b).

Plaintiff is ordered to submit by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order consistent with this tentative forthwith.

The court's order on this motion must be references and included with any subsequent motion seeking substantially similar relief.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
